Inside the body, content is arranged as a vertical stack: a short explanatory line at the top followed by a list of selected memberships (each as compact text rows or chips) and action buttons such as ‘Compare Now’ or ‘Clear Selection’ at the bottom. The body height adapts to content but is designed to be relatively compact; if many memberships are selected, an internal scroll area can appear within the panel while keeping the header visible.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves like a sidebar utility panel. On desktop, it can use a sticky behavior relative to the viewport: once the user scrolls past its initial position, the panel remains visible along the right side of the card, aligning its top margin 24–32px from the top of the viewport, while the membership-list scrolls independently on the left. On small screens, it stacks below the membership-list and loses stickiness, becoming a full-width block.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-comparison-table appears below the main white card as a separate, full-width white card when the user has selected at least two memberships to compare. It is centered in the same max-width container as the main card and aligned so its left and right edges line up exactly with the card above, reinforcing the editorial column layout. A vertical gap of about 32–40px separates the bottom of the main card (pagination area) from the top of this comparison card. Inside, there is 24px padding on all sides, and the table spans nearly the entire width, with minimal horizontal margins. Structurally, the table presents membership names as column headers across the top and features/attributes as rows down the left, forming a grid. The header row has a slightly stronger background tint to differentiate it, and the first column (feature labels) is left-aligned and visually anchored, similar to a magazine-style comparison chart. Column width is divided evenly among selected memberships, and if there are many columns, horizontal scrolling is allowed while keeping the first column sticky on the left. Vertical spacing between rows (8–12px padding) maintains clarity without feeling cramped. The overall block feels like a distinct, data-focused section that users naturally reach after exploring the list above.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears conditionally in the document flow, pushing content below it further down. It scrolls normally as part of the page. On smaller screens, the table becomes horizontally scrollable within the fixed-width card, with the container indicating overflow via subtle shadows or scroll cues.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The pagination controls sit at the bottom of the main white card, directly beneath the membership-list column and aligned with its center. There is a vertical gap of about 24–32px between the last membership-list-item and the pagination area, giving a clear end-of-list boundary. The pagination bar itself spans the full width inside the card but keeps its interactive elements (page numbers, next/previous buttons) horizontally centered for balance. A subtle top border or faint divider line can separate this band from the list above, echoing the way footer-like elements are handled in editorial layouts. Vertical padding of 12–16px around the controls maintains touch-friendly targets while preserving the spacious feel. The right sidebar column typically ends slightly above or aligned with the top of this pagination band, so the bottom edge of the main content card is visually defined by these controls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main card, serving as the natural stopping point of the primary scroll in the list area. On long lists, users scroll down to reach it; it does not stick or float.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This controller manages the visual presentation when data is loading or when filters result in no memberships. In loading state, the main card still renders its structural bands (summary bar and filter bar), but the membership-list and right-hand comparison sidebar areas are filled with skeleton placeholders. Skeleton cards for membership-list-items are stacked in the left column in the same positions and sizes as regular list items, using gray blocks for title, text, and buttons. In the right column, a simplified skeleton version of the comparison-selector-bar appears, with a solid accent header and gray boxes where selected items and buttons would be. The pagination controls are either hidden or replaced with a single-line loading indicator centered at the bottom of the list area. In empty state (no results), the left membership-list column is replaced by a centered, friendly message block that occupies approximately the top half of the list area: a headline, a short description, and a button to reset or adjust filters. This block is horizontally centered within the list column, with generous padding and whitespace around it (32–48px) to avoid looking like a missing component. The comparison sidebar remains visible and functional, but if no items are selected, it shows its default instructional content. Overall, the empty and loading visuals preserve the same two-column proportions and card framing, so the page feels consistent even without list content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Overlays or replaces content within the list and sidebar regions without altering the overall card layout. Behaves inline in the document flow; when loading completes or filters change, it transitions back to the standard list and comparison content in the same spatial positions.

name: Membership Detail
title: Membership Detail
description: Displays detailed information for a selected membership, allows configuration of billing period and quantity, and adds the membership to the shopping cart.

core_features: Load and display detailed membership information using getMembershipDetails based on the membershipId URL parameter.
Show a summary of user tools (favorites, today’s day plan, watchlist, cart) using getUserToolSummaries.
Display membership benefits, included free events per year, and descriptive terms from the membership data returned by getMembershipDetails.
Allow users to select a billing period from the billing_period_options returned by getMembershipDetails, defaulting to membership.billing_period_default.
Allow users to set a membership quantity (at least 1) before adding to cart.
Add the configured membership (membershipId, billing_period, quantity) to the cart using addMembershipToCart and display feedback based on the response.
Update cart-related indicators (e.g., cart item count) after a successful addMembershipToCart call, using getUserToolSummaries if needed.
Provide navigation back to the memberships listing and to a comparison view via URL links, and to the cart after adding an item.
user_workflows:
View membership details:
1. User opens membership_detail.html with a membershipId URL parameter.
2. Page reads membershipId from the URL.
3. membership-data-loader component calls getMembershipDetails(membershipId).
4. While loading, the page shows a loading state in detail sections.
5. On success, membership details, benefits, included_free_events_per_year_label, and billing_period_options are populated in all bound components.
6. On failure (e.g., invalid membershipId), an error message is shown and add-to-cart-related components are disabled.

Review membership benefits and terms:
1. After the membership data loads, the membership-detail-header shows key info (name, type, included free events label/value).
2. The membership-benefits-list component lists benefits from benefit_list (or from membership.benefits when present).
3. The membership-terms-section displays extended information and terms from membership.description when provided.

Select or adjust billing period:
1. After data load, billing-period-selector initializes the selectedBillingPeriod state using, in order of precedence: the billingPeriod URL parameter (if valid), otherwise membership.billing_period_default, otherwise the first value in billing_period_options.
2. The user can change the billing period by clicking or tapping an option in billing-period-selector.
3. On change, the selectedBillingPeriod state is updated, the pricing display in membership-pricing-section updates its period label, and the URL is updated to include or change the billingPeriod parameter without reloading the page.
4. If the user reloads or shares the URL, the same billingPeriod is restored from the URL.

Set quantity and add membership to cart:
1. After membership data loads, add-to-cart-form initializes quantity using, in order of precedence: the qty URL parameter (if present and valid), otherwise a default of 1.
2. The user can adjust quantity via an input control.
3. On form submission (e.g., clicking the Add to Cart button), add-to-cart-form validates that membership data is loaded, selectedBillingPeriod is set (if required), and quantity is a positive integer.
4. add-to-cart-form calls addMembershipToCart with membershipId (from page state), billing_period (selectedBillingPeriod or null if none are provided), and quantity.
5. While the request is pending, the Add to Cart button is disabled and a loading indicator is shown.
6. On success (response.success is true), add-to-cart-confirmation displays a success message and summary info derived from the returned cart object. The component may trigger a refresh of user-tools-summary-bar via getUserToolSummaries to update the cart count.
7. On failure (response.success is false), add-to-cart-confirmation displays the response.message as an error, and the Add to Cart button is re-enabled.

Go to cart after adding membership:
1. After a successful addMembershipToCart call, add-to-cart-confirmation displays actions: one to view the cart and one to continue browsing.
2. If the user selects View cart, the page navigates to cart.html with no additional parameters (or with any cart-specific parameters as defined by the broader system if needed).
3. If the user selects Continue browsing, the page navigates back to memberships.html (optionally preserving any previously used parameters if they were included in the incoming URL).

Return to memberships and comparison view without adding to cart:
1. At any time, the user can click Back to memberships in membership-navigation-links to navigate to memberships.html.
2. The user may click a comparison link in membership-navigation-links to go to a comparison view, such as memberships.html with a query parameter indicating comparison mode and the current membershipId to focus on (e.g., memberships.html?view=comparison&highlightMembershipId=<currentId>).

Use user tools summary:
1. On page load, user-tools-summary-bar calls getUserToolSummaries.
2. It displays counts for favorites_count, day_plan_today_items_count, watchlist_count, and cart_items_count.
3. After a successful addMembershipToCart call, the summary bar can be refreshed by calling getUserToolSummaries again so the cart_items_count reflects the latest cart state.
interactions:
Page load with membershipId:
- Trigger: Browser loads membership_detail.html with membershipId in the query string.
- System: Parses membershipId, billingPeriod, and qty from the URL. If membershipId is present, membership-data-loader calls getMembershipDetails. user-tools-summary-bar calls getUserToolSummaries. Components enter loading state until responses arrive.

Missing or invalid membershipId:
- Trigger: Page loads without membershipId or getMembershipDetails returns an error (e.g., membership not found or inactive).
- System: error-state-handler shows an error message indicating that this membership cannot be displayed. add-to-cart-form is disabled and does not call addMembershipToCart until a valid membershipId is present.

Change billing period:
- Trigger: User selects a different billing period option in billing-period-selector.
- System: Updates selectedBillingPeriod in page state, updates the pricing display in membership-pricing-section, and synchronizes the billingPeriod URL parameter. The Add to Cart configuration uses the updated billing period for subsequent addMembershipToCart calls.

Change quantity:
- Trigger: User modifies the quantity input in add-to-cart-form.
- System: Validates the input (must be a positive integer). On invalid input, shows inline validation and disables the Add to Cart button until fixed. On valid input, updates the quantity state used in the addMembershipToCart call.

Submit Add to Cart:
- Trigger: User clicks the Add to Cart button in add-to-cart-form.
- System: Validates that membership data is loaded, selectedBillingPeriod (if required by billing_period_options) is set, and quantity is valid. If valid, calls addMembershipToCart(membershipId, selectedBillingPeriod, quantity). While awaiting a response, disables the button and shows a loading indicator in add-to-cart-form.

Handle Add to Cart success:
- Trigger: addMembershipToCart returns with success: true.
- System: add-to-cart-confirmation displays a success state and can show information from response.cart (such as updated total_price and the specific cart item that was just added). user-tools-summary-bar may be refreshed by calling getUserToolSummaries again to update cart_items_count.

Handle Add to Cart failure:
- Trigger: addMembershipToCart returns with success: false or the call fails.
- System: add-to-cart-confirmation displays the response.message or a generic error message. The Add to Cart button is re-enabled to allow retry.

Navigate to cart:
- Trigger: User clicks a View cart action in add-to-cart-confirmation or a cart icon linked from user-tools-summary-bar.
- System: Browser navigates to cart.html.

Navigate back to memberships:
- Trigger: User clicks Back to memberships in membership-navigation-links.
- System: Browser navigates to memberships.html. If the incoming URL had parameters indicating prior list filters, those can be forwarded if present in the current URL (e.g., a preserved ref or filter parameter), but no new static values are introduced.

Open comparison view:
- Trigger: User clicks a Compare memberships action in membership-navigation-links.
- System: Browser navigates to memberships.html with parameters indicating comparison mode and optionally the current membershipId so that it is highlighted in the comparison (e.g., memberships.html?view=comparison&highlightMembershipId=<currentId>).
page_functionality.state_logic: Primary state is controlled via URL parameters and API responses. The membershipId URL parameter is required to load details; without it, the page shows an error and does not attempt getMembershipDetails. Optionally, a billingPeriod parameter in the URL preselects a billing period if it matches one of the values in billing_period_options returned from getMembershipDetails; otherwise it is ignored and the default is derived from membership.billing_period_default or the first available option. An optional qty URL parameter can prepopulate the quantity field in add-to-cart-form if it is a valid positive integer; otherwise quantity defaults to 1. On billing period changes, the page updates the billingPeriod URL parameter to reflect the new selection, enabling deep linking to a specific configuration. On navigation away and back via links, these parameters can be preserved in generated URLs if present, allowing users to return to the same configuration. No localStorage or other client-side persistence is used for state; all persistent configuration is encoded in the URL and re-derived after reloading by re-calling getMembershipDetails and getUserToolSummaries. The in-memory page state includes: loading and error flags for each API call, the loaded membership object and supplementary fields, selectedBillingPeriod, quantity, and the last add-to-cart result, which drives the state of add-to-cart-confirmation.

components.functionality: On page initialization or when the membershipId URL parameter changes, this component parses membershipId from the URL and calls getMembershipDetails to fetch membership details and configuration. It manages loading and error states and exposes the resulting data (membership, benefit_list, billing_period_options, included_free_events_per_year_label) to other components.

Displays summary counts for user tools such as favorites, today’s day plan items, watchlist items, and cart items. It calls getUserToolSummaries initially and can be refreshed after cart changes to reflect the latest counts.

Shows the primary membership summary, including membership name, membership_type, and the included free events per year using included_free_events_per_year_label and membership.included_free_events_per_year. It may also display membership.image_url when available.
Lists the membership benefits. It primarily uses the benefit_list returned from getMembershipDetails and can fall back to membership.benefits if provided, ensuring no static benefit text is embedded.

Displays the membership price and billing context. It shows membership.price combined with membership.currency and labels the price according to the currently selected billing period (e.g., annual or monthly) without hardcoding any specific text or values.

Renders available billing periods from billing_period_options and initializes the selected billing period using membership.billing_period_default or the billingPeriod URL parameter. When the user changes the selection, it updates the internal selectedBillingPeriod state and synchronizes the billingPeriod URL parameter.
Displays extended membership information and any terms or conditions contained in membership.description. It may support collapsed/expanded behavior but does not introduce any static terms.

Provides navigation links back to the memberships listing and to the comparison view. Links are generated dynamically without hardcoded identifiers, for example navigating to memberships.html or memberships.html with a query parameter indicating comparison mode and the current membershipId.

Captures the user’s purchase configuration for the membership, including billing period (from billing-period-selector) and quantity. On submission, it validates inputs and calls addMembershipToCart with membershipId, the selected billing period, and quantity. It manages loading and disabled states while the request is in progress and passes the result to add-to-cart-confirmation.

Shows success or error feedback after addMembershipToCart completes. On success, it can summarize data from the returned cart object (such as total_price and the newly added item) and present actions to view the cart or continue browsing. On error, it displays the message from the addMembershipToCart response.

Centralizes error handling for missing or invalid membershipId and failures from getMembershipDetails or addMembershipToCart. It determines when to display error messages and when to disable dependent components such as add-to-cart-form.

layout.grid_system: Appears to follow a 12-column grid with a centered max-width container. The hero content uses an approximate 8-column main area (carousel) and 4-column right sidebar (Today’s Events).
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom structure: global header (logo, location, site nav) sits above, then a slim user tools bar, followed by a single large, centered white content card on a light gray background. Inside this card, content is split into two main vertical zones on desktop: a dominant left column for the membership narrative (header, benefits, terms, related links) and a narrower right column for transactional tools (pricing, billing selector, add-to-cart, confirmation). The left column visually echoes the hero content area, while the right column plays the role of the tall sidebar like ‘TODAY’S EVENTS’. Loader and error states sit above or over this card without breaking the two-column structure.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom structure: global header (logo, location, site nav) sits above, then a slim user tools bar, followed by a single large, centered white content card on a light gray background. Inside this card, content is split into two main vertical zones on desktop: a dominant left column for the membership narrative (header, benefits, terms, related links) and a narrower right column for transactional tools (pricing, billing selector, add-to-cart, confirmation). The left column visually echoes the hero content area, while the right column plays the role of the tall sidebar like ‘TODAY’S EVENTS’. Loader and error states sit above or over this card without breaking the two-column structure.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main content card is centered with generous outer margins (roughly 56–64px top and bottom, 40–56px left and right on desktop) on a light gray background. Inside the card, the width follows a 12-column grid: approximately 8 columns (about 65–70% of the card width) for the left membership detail column and 4 columns (about 30–35%) for the right transaction sidebar. Vertical spacing between major subsections within each column follows the 24–32px pattern; internal padding within the card and sidebar is about 24px on all sides. The header/logo/nav area above remains visually light and uses about 20–25% of the initial viewport height before the main card begins.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content is moderately spacious and editorial. The membership header, key pricing info, and add-to-cart controls are grouped but surrounded by ample whitespace to keep them readable and scannable. Lists (benefits, navigation links) use compact but comfortable line spacing and 8–12px vertical padding per item, avoiding a crowded feel. Supporting text like terms is set in a narrower measure and broken into paragraphs to reduce visual heaviness. The right column is denser but still breathable, similar to the compact yet clear ‘TODAY’S EVENTS’ list in the mockup.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye lands on the global logo and location, then drops to the user tools bar, and then to the large central content card. Within the card, the eye is first drawn to the left-column membership-detail-header (name, category, hero imagery/brand area), then down through benefits. From there, attention is pulled rightward to the vertically framed pricing/add-to-cart sidebar with a subtle accent header, echoing the mockup’s ‘TODAY’S EVENTS’ panel. The confirmation banner appears at the top of this right column when active, briefly becoming the right-side focal point. Error and loading states temporarily override this flow as banners or overlays near the top center, then yield back to the main vertical-left-then-right scan pattern.

layout.overall_description: The Membership Detail page sits within a light gray canvas, with a single, large white content card centered on the screen and framed by generous margins. Above, the familiar site chrome presents the colorful logo, location tag, and simple horizontal navigation, followed by a slim user-tools bar that stretches across the page. The main card mirrors the mockup’s editorial hero-plus-sidebar structure: on the left, a wide, magazine-like column presents the membership name and key information at the top, then a clear, scannable benefits section, followed by terms and supporting links. On the right, a tall sidebar column—about one-third of the card width—groups pricing, billing options, and the add-to-cart form within a visually distinct panel that echoes the ‘TODAY’S EVENTS’ card: subtle accent color in the header, stacked content, and compact vertical rhythm. A confirmation banner appears at the top of this sidebar when the membership is added, while a loader or error banner can temporarily overlay or sit above the card content. The result feels like a clean, family-friendly editorial layout where the narrative lives on the left and the purchase tools live in a focused, helpful rail on the right.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When active, the data loader appears as a semi-transparent overlay centered over the main white content card. It dims only the card area (not the global header) with a light gray or frosted layer and places a spinner and short loading message at the horizontal and vertical center of the card. The overlay stretches edge-to-edge within the card’s bounds, respecting the card’s rounded corners, and temporarily obscures both the left content column and right sidebar while preserving the overall page framing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a fixed-position overlay anchored to the main content card, covering all underlying membership components while active and preventing interaction until loading completes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The user-tools-summary-bar is positioned directly below the global navigation and above the main white content card, spanning the full width of the centered content container. It is a low-height, horizontal strip with about 16–24px vertical padding and 24–32px side padding, matching the card’s width and alignment. Its content (e.g., sign-in status, quick links) is laid out left-to-right, with key actions right-aligned. There is roughly 16–24px of vertical space separating this bar from the top edge of the main content card, preserving the airy, layered feel of the mockup’s header region.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Stays fixed to the top of the viewport (sticky) once the user scrolls past the global header, so it remains visible while the main card scrolls beneath, similar to a subtle utility bar.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Inside the main white content card, the membership-detail-header occupies the top-left portion, aligning to the left column of the 12-column grid (approximately 8 of 12 columns, or 65–70% of card width). It sits with 24px of padding from the card’s top edge and left edge. The membership title appears prominently at the top, with any category label or small badge above or alongside it. A hero-style visual (icon, logo lockup, or banner image) can sit either behind the title as a subtle strip or directly below it, spanning the width of this left column and establishing a visual anchor similar to a simplified hero image. There is about 24–32px of vertical space separating this header block from the content that follows (benefits list). On the right side of this header row, aligned with the top of the right sidebar, the top of the pricing/add-to-cart panel visually lines up, creating a cohesive horizontal band across the card.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column at the top of the main card, forming the entry point for the detail content. It scrolls away with the page as the user moves down.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-benefits-list sits directly beneath the membership-detail-header in the left column, maintaining the same 8-column width and left alignment. It begins after roughly 24–32px of vertical whitespace to visually separate it from the header. The list is presented in a vertical stack of bullet points or compact cards, each with about 8–12px of vertical padding and 16–24px of left/right padding within the list container. The list container itself respects the card’s 24px internal padding and leaves 24–32px of space below it before the next section (pricing or terms, depending on layout decisions). On taller screens, the benefits list may extend vertically past the bottom of the right sidebar panel, but the left column maintains a clear margin to the card’s bottom edge.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows naturally in the left column below the header, expanding vertically as needed. It does not scroll independently; it scrolls with the page as a single column of content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-pricing-section is visually associated with the right sidebar but conceptually belongs to both columns. Within the right column, it appears near the top of the add-to-cart panel, just under any sidebar heading. The section occupies the full width of the 4-column sidebar and uses about 16–24px vertical padding to create a distinct block at the top of the purchase tools stack. Key price information (current price, any strike-through original price, and unit details) is shown in a larger, bolder type, aligned left within the sidebar. On narrow viewports where the layout stacks, this section moves above the add-to-cart form but remains grouped with it, spanning nearly 100% of the card width with 24px side padding. Visually, it functions much like the leading event entry in the mockup’s sidebar: a highlighted, easy-to-scan summary at the top of a vertical list of controls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sits at the top of the right sidebar’s content stack, immediately below the sidebar’s title area. It is static within the sidebar and scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The billing-period-selector is placed in the right sidebar directly below the membership-pricing-section, separated by approximately 16–24px of vertical space. It occupies the full width of the 4-column sidebar. Options (monthly, yearly, etc.) are displayed as a compact horizontal pill group or a vertical radio list, with individual options using around 8–12px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding to echo the mockup’s simple textual nav elements. The selector maintains a tight, module-like presence to keep the right column focused and functional. It sits above the add-to-cart form, guiding the user to choose a billing period before specifying quantity or adding to cart.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right sidebar’s vertical stack, flowing between the pricing section above and add-to-cart form below. On mobile, it appears directly above the add-to-cart form in the single-column flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-terms-section lives in the left column beneath the benefits list. After a 24–32px vertical gap below the last benefit item, the terms section spans the same 8-column width with a clear section heading and paragraphs of text. The text block uses a narrower effective line length (by adding internal side padding or slightly reduced width) to maintain readability and prevent a wall of text. Paragraphs are separated by 8–12px of space, and the entire section maintains at least 24px of padding from the left and right internal edges of the card. There is about 24–32px of space below the terms section before the membership-navigation-links, giving the bottom of the card a light, open feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows as a standard text block in the left column, expanding vertically as needed, scrolling away with the page. It does not overlap or float; it respects the card’s padding and bottom margin.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-navigation-links component is positioned at the bottom of the left column, directly beneath the membership-terms-section and separated from it by approximately 24px of vertical space. It spans the same 8-column width and is aligned to the left margin, with individual links styled as simple textual items similar to the main site navigation—small vertical padding (6–10px) per item and 16–24px horizontal padding within the link group. Depending on content, the links may appear as a horizontal row (e.g., 'Back to memberships', 'View all plans') or a short vertical list. This area sits slightly above the bottom of the card, leaving 24–32px of white space between the last link and the card’s bottom edge, echoing the mockup’s spacious margins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the lower end of the left column. On smaller screens, it appears below all primary content (benefits, terms) in the single-column stack, forming the natural exit point of the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The add-to-cart-form occupies the lower half of the right sidebar column, immediately below the billing-period-selector. It fills the width of the 4-column sidebar and is contained within a visually grouped panel that shares the sidebar’s 24px padding. Within this panel, the quantity field, any notes, and the main add-to-cart button are stacked vertically with about 12–16px between fields and a slightly larger gap (16–24px) above the primary button. The primary button is full-width within the sidebar, aligned left, and serves as a strong call-to-action akin to the emphasis of items in the mockup’s vertical events list. There is at least 24px of padding between the bottom of the form and the card’s bottom edge, ensuring the sidebar doesn’t feel cramped even when content is tall.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right sidebar. On wider screens, it stays alongside the left column as the user scrolls. Optionally, on tall viewports, the entire sidebar (including the form) can be made sticky within the card boundaries so it remains visible while the left column scrolls; if implemented, it sticks relative to the top of the main card with a small offset.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When visible, the add-to-cart-confirmation appears inside the main content card at the top of the right sidebar column, just above the membership-pricing-section. It is styled as a horizontal notification banner that spans the full width of the sidebar (4 columns) with a lightly tinted background and subtle border, using 12–16px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding. The banner sits flush with the sidebar’s internal padding and pushes the rest of the sidebar content (pricing, billing selector, add-to-cart form) downward, preserving the column’s vertical rhythm. On smaller screens where the layout stacks, this banner appears directly above the pricing section in the single-column flow, still just above the price and form but below the main membership header.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, non-sticky banner within the right column’s content stack. It appears and disappears without changing the overall layout structure, simply adjusting the vertical position of the sidebar content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error-state-handler presents errors in a way that respects the editorial layout. Global or major errors (e.g., failure to load membership data) appear as a prominent banner at the top of the main white content card, spanning the full card width (all 12 columns) and sitting just below the card’s top padding and above the membership-detail-header. This banner uses a strong accent color bar on the left and 12–16px of vertical padding to ensure visibility while still aligned with the card’s internal padding. Inline errors related to specific actions (e.g., add-to-cart form validation) appear closer to their relevant components: for example, a small error message block directly above or below the problematic field within the add-to-cart-form, or within the right sidebar, without breaking the column structure. There is always at least 8–12px of space separating error messages from surrounding elements to avoid visual clutter.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears either as an inline banner anchored to the top of the card (for page-level errors) or as localized inline messages attached to specific components (for form/interaction errors). Error banners scroll with the page and do not fixed-position overlay content, keeping the layout consistent with the editorial feel.

name: Cart
title: Cart
description: Displays memberships currently in the cart, allows simple adjustments, and lets the user confirm their selection before payment.

core_features: Load and display the current cart contents (memberships, quantities, billing periods, and prices) using getCart.
Show user tool counts (favorites, day plan today, watchlist, cart) in the header using getUserToolSummaries.
Allow changing the billing period for individual cart items (where applicable) using updateCartItemBillingPeriod.
Allow removing memberships from the cart using removeCartItem, updating totals accordingly.
Provide navigation to membership_detail.html for each membership via its name, passing membershipId as a URL parameter.
Provide navigation back to memberships.html via a 'Continue browsing memberships' action.
Allow the user to confirm the cart selection (without payment) using confirmCartSelection and show a confirmation state on the page.
user_workflows: View Cart: The user opens cart.html from the header. On page load, the app parses any URL parameters (e.g., status, cartId), calls getCart to retrieve the current cart contents, and calls getUserToolSummaries to display user-tool counts. The cart items list, totals, and confirmation/edit state are rendered based on this data.

Change Billing Period: For a cart item that supports multiple billing periods, the user opens a billing period selector (for example, a dropdown). When the user selects a different billing period, the app calls updateCartItemBillingPeriod with the cartItemId and the newly selected billing_period. On success, the returned cart object replaces the current cart state so item pricing and totals are updated immediately.

Remove an Item: The user clicks a 'Remove' action on a cart item. The app calls removeCartItem with the item's cartItemId. On success, the returned cart object is used to refresh the items list and totals. If no items remain, the cart empty state is shown along with a link to memberships.html.

Review Membership Details: The user clicks on a membership name in the cart items list. The app navigates to membership_detail.html passing the membership's identifier as a URL parameter (membership_detail.html?membershipId={membership_id}). When the user is done, they can navigate back to cart.html via the site navigation.

Confirm Cart Selection: The user reviews all items and clicks the primary confirm/proceed button. The app calls confirmCartSelection (with no parameters). On success, the app: (1) updates an in-memory page state flag indicating the cart is confirmed, (2) may update the cart.html URL to include status=confirmed and cartId={response.cart_id}, and (3) shows a confirmation banner or panel and disables further editing actions (billing period changes, remove actions, and the confirm button) for the current view.

Continue Browsing Memberships: From either the filled cart or empty cart state, the user clicks 'Continue browsing memberships'. The app navigates to memberships.html (e.g., memberships.html) so the user can add more memberships. Returning to cart.html will again use getCart to show the updated cart.
interactions: Initial Load: When cart.html is opened or refreshed, the app parses URL parameters (e.g., status, cartId) and triggers getCart and getUserToolSummaries. The responses populate the cart items list, totals, header tool counts, and determine whether confirmation/edit controls are active.

Click Membership Name: Clicking a membership name in the cart triggers navigation to membership_detail.html with membershipId set to the item's membership_id (membership_detail.html?membershipId={membership_id}).

Change Billing Period: Changing a billing period selector for a cart item triggers an onChange handler that calls updateCartItemBillingPeriod(cartItemId, billing_period). On a successful response, the cart state (items and total_price) is replaced with the returned cart object.

Remove Item: Clicking a cart item 'Remove' button triggers an onClick handler that calls removeCartItem(cartItemId). On success, the cart items list and totals are re-rendered from the returned cart object. If the items array is empty, the items list component hides and the empty-state component shows.

Confirm Selection Button: Clicking the 'Confirm selection' or 'Proceed' button triggers an onClick handler that calls confirmCartSelection(). While the request is pending, the button can be shown as disabled with a loading state. On success, the page updates an internal confirmation state (e.g., confirmed = true, confirmedAt = response.confirmed_at), updates the URL to include status=confirmed and cartId={response.cart_id} if not already present, and displays a confirmation banner. Edit actions are disabled for this view.

Continue Browsing Link: Clicking the 'Continue browsing memberships' link triggers navigation to memberships.html. This interaction is available both from the main cart summary and from the empty cart state.

Header Cart Icon / User Tools: The header cart link or icon navigates to cart.html. The visual badge on this icon uses cart_items_count from getUserToolSummaries, but the only behavior specific to this page is navigation to cart.html.
page_functionality.state_logic: The cart page uses URL parameters and API responses (not localStorage) to manage state. The base URL is cart.html. Optional query parameters are: (1) status: controls how the cart is presented. When status=confirmed, the page treats the cart as confirmed for the current view and disables edit/confirm actions. If status is missing or has any other value, the cart is treated as editable. This parameter is typically set after a successful confirmCartSelection call (e.g., cart.html?status=confirmed&cartId={response.cart_id}). (2) cartId: an optional identifier passed in the URL (e.g., cart.html?cartId={id}). The getCart interface does not accept parameters and always returns the current active cart; however, cartId in the URL is used by the front-end only for consistency and cross-page navigation, for example when redirecting back from another page after confirmation. On each load, the page: (a) reads status and cartId from the URL; (b) calls getCart and uses the returned cart_id to verify or overwrite any cartId indicated by the URL for the current view; (c) sets a derived UI state such as isEditable = (status !== 'confirmed') && (cart.items length > 0). The confirmation timestamp (confirmed_at) received from confirmCartSelection is kept only in in-memory state for the current tab/session and is not persisted in the URL or storage; if the page is reloaded, confirmation status is inferred purely from the status URL parameter and not from any previously stored value. All pricing, currency, and membership details shown on the page are bound directly to the latest getCart or updateCartItemBillingPeriod/removeCartItem responses to avoid any hardcoded or static values.

components.functionality: On page load, parse URL parameters (status, cartId), fetch the current cart using getCart, fetch user tool summaries using getUserToolSummaries, and derive initial UI state (editable vs. confirmed, empty vs. non-empty). This component orchestrates the first render of child components.

Display summary counts for favorites, today's day plan items, watchlist items, and cart items using getUserToolSummaries. Update the visible badge on the cart link in the header and provide navigation to cart.html when the cart tool is clicked.

Render a list of all cart items returned by getCart (or updated cart responses). For each item, show membership name (as a link to membership_detail.html?membershipId={membership_id}), current billing period, quantity, unit price, line total, and actions to change the billing period or remove the item. This component hides when there are no items.
data_binding: CartItem (cart_item_id, membership_id, membership_name, billing_period, quantity, price_per_unit, line_total)

components.functionality: For an individual cart item, provide a control (such as a dropdown) for selecting the billing period (e.g., monthly or annual) when the membership supports more than one billing option. When the user changes the selection, call updateCartItemBillingPeriod and update the displayed pricing and totals according to the returned cart data.

Provide a remove action (button or link) for each cart item. When activated, call removeCartItem with the specific cartItemId and then refresh the cart items list and totals based on the returned cart. If the resulting cart has no items, trigger the empty-cart state display.

Display the cart total (total_price and currency from the latest cart object) and provide primary actions such as 'Confirm selection' and 'Continue browsing memberships'. The confirm button is enabled only when the cart is non-empty and the page is in editable status. On confirm, this component triggers confirmCartSelection and, on success, switches the page to a confirmed state.
data_binding: ConfirmationState (derived from URL status and confirmCartSelection response)

components.functionality: When the cart is in a confirmed state (either the current view has just received a successful response from confirmCartSelection or the URL includes status=confirmed), display a confirmation message describing that the memberships have been confirmed. If confirmCartSelection was just called in this view, also display the confirmation timestamp (confirmed_at) from the response. While this banner is active, editing controls (billing period changes, remove item, confirm button) are disabled.

If getCart (or updated cart responses) indicate that there are no items in the cart, hide the cart-items-list and cart-summary sections, and display a simple empty-state message with a 'Continue browsing memberships' link that navigates to memberships.html.

Manage all navigation triggered from this page: (1) membership detail navigation from a cart item (membership_detail.html?membershipId={membership_id}); (2) continue browsing navigation to memberships.html; (3) optional URL update after confirmation (e.g., appending or updating status=confirmed and cartId={response.cart_id} on cart.html). This component centralizes URL generation and updates based on current data.

layout.grid_system: Appears to follow a 12-column grid with a centered max-width container. The hero content uses an approximate 8-column main area (carousel) and 4-column right sidebar (Today’s Events).
layout.layout_pattern: The cart page mirrors the mockup’s linear, editorial flow: a light gray page background with a centered, fixed-width content column. At the top sits a slim user tools bar, then the existing brand header (logo/location, assumed globally), followed by a single-line horizontal navigation row. Beneath this, a large white primary content card fills most of the viewport height. Inside that card, the layout splits into a two-column band: the left 2/3 is the cart items area (replacing the hero carousel), and the right 1/3 is a vertical cart summary sidebar (taking the role of the 'TODAY'S EVENTS' panel). A confirmation banner, when present, runs full-width across the top of the card above the two-column content. Empty state content replaces the left column list when the cart is empty, while the summary adjusts accordingly.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The cart page mirrors the mockup’s linear, editorial flow: a light gray page background with a centered, fixed-width content column. At the top sits a slim user tools bar, then the existing brand header (logo/location, assumed globally), followed by a single-line horizontal navigation row. Beneath this, a large white primary content card fills most of the viewport height. Inside that card, the layout splits into a two-column band: the left 2/3 is the cart items area (replacing the hero carousel), and the right 1/3 is a vertical cart summary sidebar (taking the role of the 'TODAY'S EVENTS' panel). A confirmation banner, when present, runs full-width across the top of the card above the two-column content. Empty state content replaces the left column list when the cart is empty, while the summary adjusts accordingly.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The page uses a centered max-width container (~1120–1200px) on desktop, with generous outer margins (40–64px) of light gray background around the main card. The header (user tools + nav) consumes about the top 20–25% of the viewport with generous vertical spacing but light content. The main white card then occupies the central 60–70% of the viewport height. Within the card, roughly 65–70% of the horizontal width is dedicated to the left cart items column (approximately 8 of 12 grid columns) and 30–35% to the right summary sidebar (approximately 4 of 12 columns). The card’s internal padding is around 24–32px on all sides, and list rows and controls respect the 8px spacing scale.
layout_strategies.content_density: The page maintains a moderately spacious feel. The cart items list is structured as a vertical stack of clearly separated rows with white space between them, avoiding a cramped table look. Each row uses 16–24px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, with 8–16px gaps between rows. The summary sidebar is compact but readable, with modest spacing between line items and a clear visual hierarchy for subtotals and totals. The confirmation banner and empty state are given extra breathing room above and below to keep them feeling prominent and friendly rather than crowded.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye moves vertically from the user tools bar and brand header down to the navigation, then into the main white card. Within the card, the confirmation banner (if present) captures attention first. From there, the primary flow drops into the left cart items column, where users scan from top to bottom through their selected memberships. The right cart summary panel, with its colored header bar echoing the 'TODAY'S EVENTS' style, creates a strong secondary pull to the right. The user naturally oscillates between the details on the left (items, billing period, removal) and the summary and primary call-to-action on the right. In an empty state, the central empty-state content becomes the primary focal point, with the right sidebar visually toned down.

layout.overall_description: The cart page sits on a soft light gray background, with all functional content collected into a centered, magazine-like column. At the very top, a slim, right-aligned user tools bar provides quick account/cart context. Below the global logo and location (assumed above), a single-line horizontal navigation row spans the width of the central container, maintaining the editorial feel of the site. A large white card then dominates the midsection of the page, framed by generous gray margins on all sides. Inside this card, a tinted confirmation banner may stretch across the top, followed by a two-column content band. On the left, a spacious vertical stack of cart items occupies about two-thirds of the card, each item laid out like its own mini card with a clear title, details, billing period control, price, and a neatly placed remove link at the far right. On the right, a taller, narrower summary panel with a bold colored header bar mirrors the visual weight of the original 'TODAY'S EVENTS' sidebar, listing subtotals, discounts, and a prominent checkout button. The overall effect is a clear, family-friendly, editorial layout where the main reading flow is left column down, with frequent glances to the right column to verify totals, all framed by soft whitespace that keeps the experience calm and easy to scan.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart page initialization defines the overall canvas: a full-viewport light gray background (covering 100% width and height), with a centered max-width content container (around 1120–1200px wide) aligned in the middle of the page horizontally. This container begins near the top of the viewport with a comfortable top margin (40–64px) from the browser edge. All subsequent components (user tools bar, navigation, and the main cart card) are vertically stacked within this container, following a clean top-to-bottom flow. The main cart content is encapsulated in a prominent white card that sits roughly one section (24–32px) below the navigation and is vertically padded (24–32px all around) inside its border, clearly framed against the light gray background.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Acts as the static page-level wrapper and grid system; it scrolls normally with the page and sets the 12-column grid and max-width for all inner components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The user tools summary bar is a slim horizontal strip at the very top of the central content container, sitting above the navigation area and any existing logo/location header. It spans the full width of the container but has a shallow height, using approximately 8–12px of vertical padding above and below its contents. Content inside the bar is right-aligned, with user-related controls (e.g., sign-in state, a tiny cart or profile link) grouped together and spaced using 16–24px gaps. There is generous whitespace above (16–24px to the top of the viewport) and below (16–24px before the navigation area) to keep it light and unobtrusive. The bar visually aligns with the editorial style by keeping typography simple and subtle, acting as a tertiary information band rather than a heavy app-like toolbar.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrolling with the page, sitting at the top of the main content container without fixed or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The navigation links form a single-line horizontal nav row placed below the brand header (logo and location, assumed globally) and just under the user tools bar. It spans nearly the full width of the central container, aligned centrally on the page, with equal left and right padding (24–32px) relative to the container edges. The nav items are laid out in a horizontal line, centered or slightly left-aligned, each with 8–12px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal spacing between links. The nav row sits with about 16–24px of vertical space above and below it, creating a clear breathing zone before the main content card begins. Visually, it reads as a thin, editorial masthead navigation, with light typography and plenty of whitespace, anchoring the top of the page content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the document flow; it scrolls away with the rest of the content and does not stick to the top of the viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart confirmation banner is a full-width strip positioned at the very top inside the main white content card, directly beneath the card’s top padding and above the two-column cart layout. It stretches across the entire internal width of the card (matching the 12-column grid span) and uses a lightly tinted background in a friendly brand color (e.g., soft green or a brand pink variant). The banner is about the height of one to one-and-a-half lines of text with 12–16px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding. There is a comfortable 16–24px gap below the banner before the two-column content begins. When present, it becomes a horizontal highlight that the eye lands on immediately upon entering the card; when absent, the cart items and summary columns shift up to occupy the space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Embedded at the top of the card’s content; appears conditionally and pushes subsequent content downward in the normal flow, with no fixed or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart items list occupies the left main column inside the white content card, mirroring the width of the original hero area. It spans approximately 65–70% of the card’s internal width, roughly equivalent to 8 of 12 grid columns, and extends vertically from just below the confirmation banner (or top-of-card padding) down to near the bottom of the card. This column begins with a subtle section heading (e.g., "Your Cart"), then flows into a vertical stack of item rows. Each item row is rendered as a white sub-card or delineated row with a light bottom border or soft shadow, using 16–24px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding. Within each row, the item title and description are left-aligned, occupying the left 60–70% of the row, while pricing, billing period control, and remove control occupy the right 30–40%, aligned to the right edge. Rows are separated by 8–16px of vertical gap to retain a spacious, readable feel. The entire list aligns to the top of the left column, creating a strong vertical reading path down the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column; it scrolls normally inside the page. The list expands vertically as more items are added, pushing the card’s bottom edge down as needed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart billing period control is positioned within each cart item row, in the right-hand portion of the row, just to the left of or directly under the item’s price. Horizontally, the row can be imagined as divided into three bands: left (item info, ~60%), middle (billing period, ~20%), and right (price and remove, ~20%). The billing period control sits in the middle band, aligned vertically with the main text of the row so that it feels integrated rather than secondary. It is spaced 8–12px to the right of the item description and 8–12px to the left of the price, with 4–8px of padding inside the control itself, following the base spacing scale. On taller rows, the control is vertically centered in the row; on more compact rows, it aligns with the baseline of the price text. Overall, it is clearly visible but subordinate to the item name and price, behaving like an inline, magazine-style filter or selector rather than a bulky app widget.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline element within each cart item row, flowing with the row’s layout; no independent scrolling or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart remove item control is placed at the far right edge of each cart item row, visually aligned with the item title and price. It typically appears as a small text link or icon (e.g., "Remove") that sits either directly to the right of the price or slightly below it, right-aligned with the row’s outer edge. Horizontally, it occupies only a narrow slice (about 10–15% of the row width), with a 8–12px gap from the price to prevent crowding. Vertically, it is aligned to the top or middle of the row so it is easily scannable when moving down the list. The control uses minimal padding (4–8px click target padding around the text or icon) and is visually lightweight, so it doesn’t compete with the title or total price, but remains discoverable at the end of each row’s reading path.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, right-aligned control within each cart item row; part of the normal flow with no special positioning beyond alignment within its parent row.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart summary panel sits on the right side of the main white content card, aligning to the top edge of the cart items list to form a clear two-column band. It occupies roughly 30–35% of the card’s width (about 4 of 12 grid columns), and extends vertically as needed to contain order details and the checkout action. The panel itself is a nested white card or bordered panel with about 24px internal padding and a visually distinctive header bar across the top, echoing the bold pink 'TODAY'S EVENTS' header from the mockup. This header bar spans the full width of the panel, is 40–56px tall, and contains the panel title (e.g., "Order Summary"), with 12–16px vertical padding. Below the header, line items (subtotal, discounts, tax, total) are stacked with 8–12px vertical spacing and clear right-aligned values. A prominent primary button near the bottom spans most of the panel width (70–90%), centered or full-width within the panel. The panel maintains at least 24–32px of horizontal gap from the left column to avoid feeling cramped, and its top aligns with the first row of cart items to keep the layout visually balanced.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static sidebar within the main card; it scrolls with the page and remains aligned to the right side of the card without sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The empty state replaces the cart items list area when there are no memberships in the cart. It is positioned in the same left column region that the cart-items-list would normally occupy, starting at the same top alignment within the card. Instead of multiple rows, a single, centered empty-state block fills this area: a simple illustration or icon at the top, a short headline, and a brief explanatory sentence, followed by a primary or secondary call-to-action button encouraging users to browse memberships or events. Horizontally, the empty-state content is centered within the left column (which still spans about 65–70% of the card width), with ample side padding (24–32px) to match the card’s internal spacing. Vertically, there is generous whitespace above and below the empty-state content (32–48px), so it feels airy and inviting. The right summary panel may collapse into a smaller message (e.g., just stating "Your cart is empty") or remain but visually de-emphasized, ensuring the empty state in the left column is the clear focal point.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally replaces the cart-items-list within the left column; it is static and scrolls with the page, expanding or contracting its vertical space based on content but always anchored in the left column area.

name: Registration
title: Activity Registration
description: Page for registering participants for a specific activity session, including session selection, participant details, and registration confirmation.

core_features: Read activityId and optional sessionId from the page URL to determine which activity and session the registration is for.

Fetch registration context for the selected activity using getRegistrationFormDefaults (activity summary, session options, default session, default participant count, price per participant, currency, and whether registration is open).
Fetch user tool summary counts (favorites, today’s day plan items, watchlist, cart) using getUserToolSummaries to display in a compact user tools bar.

Display an activity summary block showing the selected activity’s title, recommended age range, and price information using activity_summary and price_per_participant from getRegistrationFormDefaults.
Display a session selector (dropdown or equivalent) populated from session_options, with default selection controlled by default_session_id or URL sessionId when present.
Show capacity and registration-availability indicators for the selected session based on ActivitySession fields (capacity_total, capacity_available, is_registration_open).
Render a registration form with fields for number of participants, parent/guardian name, contact phone, and optional contact email, prefilled with default_participants_count when provided.
Validate required fields and basic constraints (e.g., participants_count must be positive and not exceed capacity_available when that data exists) before calling submitRegistration.
Submit the registration via submitRegistration (activityId, sessionId, participants_count, parent_guardian_name, contact_phone, contact_email) when the user clicks the Confirm Registration button.
Handle submitRegistration results: on success, show a confirmation state with details from the returned Registration object; on failure, show an error message and keep the form state intact.
Provide a 'Back to activity' navigation link that returns the user to activity_detail.html with the correct activity id in the URL.
Provide an 'Add to day plan' navigation link after a successful registration that sends the user to day_plan.html with a date parameter derived from the selected session’s start_datetime.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1 – Load registration page from activity detail:
1. User clicks a 'Register' or 'Book Now' control on activity_detail.html.
2. Browser navigates to registration.html with activityId in the URL and optional sessionId if the user had selected a specific time slot.
3. On page load, the controller reads activityId and sessionId from the URL.
4. The page calls getRegistrationFormDefaults with activityId and the URL sessionId (if present).
5. The page also calls getUserToolSummaries to populate the user tools summary bar.
6. Once getRegistrationFormDefaults returns, the page displays the activity summary, session selector (with default session), and the prefilled registration form.
7. User reviews the summary and edits participant and contact information as needed.

Workflow 2 – Change session selection before registering:
1. After the initial load, the user opens the session selector component.
2. User chooses a different session from session_options.
3. The selectedSessionId state is updated to the newly chosen session’s id.
4. The page optionally updates the URL’s sessionId parameter (e.g., via history APIs) so that the selected session is preserved if the page is reloaded or shared.
5. The activity summary panel and any capacity/availability indicators update to reflect the newly selected session.
6. The registration form remains filled with the user’s input; only session-related details (such as date/time display and capacity-related validation) adjust to the new session.

Workflow 3 – Submit registration and view confirmation:
1. User fills in or confirms participants_count, parent_guardian_name, contact_phone, and optionally contact_email.
2. User clicks the Confirm Registration button.
3. Client-side validation runs; if required fields are missing or invalid, validation error messages are shown and submission is blocked.
4. If validation passes and registration is open for the selected session, the page calls submitRegistration with activityId, selectedSessionId, participants_count, parent_guardian_name, contact_phone, and contact_email.
5. While waiting for the API response, the form is disabled and a submitting state is shown.
6. If submitRegistration returns success = true, the page stores the returned registration object, switches to a confirmation state, and hides or disables the form.
7. The confirmation panel shows registration details (e.g., participants_count, registration_status, total_price, currency, and session date/time resolved from the selected ActivitySession).
8. Within the confirmation panel, the user can click 'Back to activity' to navigate to activity_detail.html?id={activity_id} or 'Add to day plan' to navigate to day_plan.html?date={sessionDateDerivedFromSelectedSession}.

Workflow 4 – Handle unavailable or closed sessions:
1. During initial load or session selection changes, the page evaluates the selected session’s ActivitySession fields: capacity_available and is_registration_open.
2. If is_registration_open is false or capacity_available is present and indicates no availability, the form’s Confirm Registration button is disabled and a message is shown explaining that registration for this session is not available.
3. If other sessions in session_options are available, the user can select a different session from the session selector; the form becomes enabled again when a valid, open session is selected.
4. If no sessions are open for registration, the page keeps the form disabled and only displays the non-interactive session information and a message indicating that registrations are closed.

Workflow 5 – Error handling and retry:
1. If getRegistrationFormDefaults fails (e.g., due to an invalid activityId or technical error), the page shows an error state and hides the registration form and confirmation panel.
2. If submitRegistration returns success = false, the page displays the returned message to the user, keeps the form visible with all previously entered values, and allows the user to correct the issues and try again.
3. The user can modify participants_count, contact details, or choose a different session, and then click Confirm Registration again to resubmit.
interactions:
Interaction – Page load:
- Trigger: Browser loads registration.html.
- Behavior: Read activityId and sessionId from the URL query parameters. If activityId is present, call getRegistrationFormDefaults(activityId, sessionId) and getUserToolSummaries(). If activityId is missing, show an error message and do not render the form.

Interaction – Session selection change:
- Trigger: User selects a different session in the session selector component.
- Behavior: Update internal selectedSessionId state. Optionally update the URL’s sessionId parameter so that it matches the currently selected session. Recalculate any capacity-related validation constraints. Update displayed session date/time and capacity status in the activity summary panel. Keep existing form field inputs.

Interaction – Participants count input:
- Trigger: User changes the participants_count field.
- Behavior: Update participants_count in the form state. Run inline validation (e.g., must be greater than zero and, when capacity_available is known, must not exceed capacity_available). If invalid, display an inline error and disable the Confirm Registration button until resolved.

Interaction – Parent/guardian and contact inputs:
- Trigger: User types into parent_guardian_name, contact_phone, or contact_email fields.
- Behavior: Update corresponding fields in the form state. Validate required fields (parent_guardian_name and contact_phone) and basic formatting rules for contact_phone and contact_email. Show inline errors when invalid.

Interaction – Confirm Registration button click:
- Trigger: User clicks the Confirm Registration button.
- Behavior: Run full form validation. If invalid, prevent submission and show all relevant validation errors. If valid and registration is open for the selected session, call submitRegistration with the current form values and selectedSessionId. Set a submitting state to disable inputs and show progress until the API responds.

Interaction – SubmitRegistration response handling:
- Trigger: Response received from submitRegistration.
- Behavior (success = true): Store the returned registration object in state, set registration_status to the value from the response, switch the visible UI from form to confirmation panel, and show the success message from the API along with registration details. Behavior (success = false): Show the message from the API as an error, keep the form visible with values intact, and clear the submitting state so the user can retry.

Interaction – Back to activity link click:
- Trigger: User clicks 'Back to activity' in the confirmation panel.
- Behavior: Navigate to activity_detail.html with id set to the current activityId using URL query parameters (e.g., activity_detail.html?id={activityId}).

Interaction – Add to day plan link click:
- Trigger: User clicks 'Add to day plan' in the confirmation panel.
- Behavior: Derive the session date from the selected ActivitySession’s start_datetime (converted to a date format consistent with the site’s routing). Navigate to day_plan.html with date set to this derived value (e.g., day_plan.html?date={sessionDate}).

Interaction – URL parameter change (internal navigation):
- Trigger: URL query parameters change while staying on registration.html (for example, client-side code updates sessionId to match selectedSessionId).
- Behavior: The controller listens for URL changes; when sessionId changes, it updates internal state (selectedSessionId) to match and rebinds session-specific display data (date/time, capacity). ActivityId is assumed constant for this page instance; a change to activityId would trigger a full reinitialization via getRegistrationFormDefaults.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The registration page uses URL parameters and in-page state together to control behavior. On initial load, the page reads activityId and optional sessionId from the URL query string. If activityId is not present, the page enters an error state and does not call getRegistrationFormDefaults. If activityId is present, the page calls getRegistrationFormDefaults(activityId, sessionId) and getUserToolSummaries() and initializes the following internal states: activitySummary (from activity_summary), sessionOptions (from session_options), selectedSessionId (from default_session_id, overridden by URL sessionId if provided and valid), defaultParticipantsCount (from default_participants_count), pricePerParticipant, currency, and registrationOpen (from registration_open). The selected session is determined by matching selectedSessionId against sessionOptions; when selectedSessionId does not match any option, the page falls back to default_session_id.

The URL’s sessionId parameter is the canonical shareable representation of the chosen session. When the user changes the session via the UI, the page updates selectedSessionId and may also update the URL’s sessionId parameter using client-side navigation APIs without reloading the page. If the page is reloaded or accessed via a shared link that includes both activityId and sessionId, the same getRegistrationFormDefaults call is made and the same session is preselected.

Form state (participants_count, parent_guardian_name, contact_phone, contact_email) and transient UI states (loading, submit in progress, validation errors, confirmation visible) are maintained in memory for the duration of the page session and are not persisted in URL parameters. The confirmation state is only entered after submitRegistration returns success = true. Once in the confirmation state, the registration object from the response is treated as the authoritative record of the registration, including registration_status, participants_count, total_price, and currency.

Outgoing navigation uses URL parameters to transfer context: the 'Back to activity' link always uses the current activityId (unchanged from the page’s initial activityId) as the id parameter for activity_detail.html. The 'Add to day plan' link derives a sessionDate from the selected ActivitySession’s start_datetime (using a consistent date-only format) and passes it as the date parameter to day_plan.html. No page state is stored in localStorage; all cross-page state is conveyed through URL parameters and the backend data models exposed by the existing interfaces.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial data loading, global state management, and interaction between the registration form, session selection, activity summary, confirmation panel, and navigation. On load, reads activityId and sessionId from the URL and calls getRegistrationFormDefaults and getUserToolSummaries. Maintains internal state for activitySummary, sessionOptions, selectedSessionId, form values, validation errors, loading and submitting flags, registrationOpen, and the resulting registration object after a successful submission.

Displays compact counts for user tools (favorites, today’s day plan items, watchlist, cart) based on data from getUserToolSummaries. Can be used as part of the site header or a page-level utility strip. The component is read-only on this page and serves as informational context while the user registers for an activity.

Shows key information about the activity and currently selected session. Binds to activity_summary for the title, recommended age range (min_age, max_age), and pricing (price_type, price_min, price_currency, price_unit, price_per_participant). Also uses the selected ActivitySession (resolved from sessionOptions and selectedSessionId) to display session-specific details such as start_datetime, end_datetime when available, day_of_week, is_weekend, and any capacity-related information (capacity_total, capacity_available, is_registration_open). Updates in response to session selection changes.

Provides a control (e.g., dropdown or list) listing all session_options returned by getRegistrationFormDefaults. Each option corresponds to an ActivitySession. The selector highlights the current selectedSessionId and allows the user to change it. Sessions with no capacity_available or with is_registration_open = false can be indicated as unavailable. When the user selects a new session, the component updates selectedSessionId in the controller and may trigger a URL sessionId update.

Renders and manages the registration form fields: participants_count, parent_guardian_name, contact_phone, and optional contact_email. Initializes participants_count with default_participants_count when available from getRegistrationFormDefaults. Performs client-side validation (required fields, numeric ranges, and capacity checks relative to the selected ActivitySession’s capacity_available when present). Enables or disables the Confirm Registration button based on validation and registrationOpen state. On submit, calls submitRegistration with the current form values and selectedSessionId, and coordinates submission state (disabled inputs and progress indicators).

Displays contextual messages about the registration state, including loading indicators while getRegistrationFormDefaults is in progress, error messages if the activity cannot be loaded, notices when registration is closed or the selected session is full, and errors returned by submitRegistration when success = false. This component subscribes to state changes in the controller and shows or hides messages accordingly.

Visible only after a successful submitRegistration call (success = true). Uses the returned Registration object combined with the current activitySummary and selected ActivitySession to display confirmation details: registration_status, participants_count, total_price, currency, and the specific session date and time. Provides two navigation actions: 'Back to activity', which links to activity_detail.html?id={activity_id}, and 'Add to day plan', which links to day_plan.html?date={sessionDateDerivedFromSelectedSession.start_datetime}. Hides or replaces the registration form when active.

layout.grid_system: Appears to follow a 12-column grid with a centered max-width container. The hero content uses an approximate 8-column main area (carousel) and 4-column right sidebar (Today’s Events).
layout.layout_pattern: Use the same linear, top-to-bottom sequencing as the mockup: global site header (logo/location/nav, outside this spec) → centered registration card. Inside the card, follow the 2-column band pattern: an 8-column left main area for the activity context, session selection, and the registration form; and a 4-column right sidebar for registration status and confirmation. Above the split, run a slim, full-width tools bar under a local page header provided by the page controller, echoing the mockup’s logo/location/nav hierarchy with a page-title/tools hierarchy instead.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use the same linear, top-to-bottom sequencing as the mockup: global site header (logo/location/nav, outside this spec) → centered registration card. Inside the card, follow the 2-column band pattern: an 8-column left main area for the activity context, session selection, and the registration form; and a 4-column right sidebar for registration status and confirmation. Above the split, run a slim, full-width tools bar under a local page header provided by the page controller, echoing the mockup’s logo/location/nav hierarchy with a page-title/tools hierarchy instead.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Keep a fixed, centered max-width container with generous margins on the light gray page background. The registration card spans roughly 80–90% of the container width, with 24px inner padding. Top header zone (page title + tools) takes the upper 20–25% of the card’s height on first load; the remaining 75–80% is devoted to the 2-column content band. Within that band, allocate about 65–70% of the width to the left column (activity summary, session selector, form) and 30–35% to the right column (status and confirmation), mirroring the hero/‘Today’s Events’ split.
